summary: Good‑quality soundboard recording of a transitional early‑70s show, highlighted by a stellar “Looks Like Rain” with pedal steel and powerhouse blues numbers.
review: The show captures the Grateful Dead in an exciting period of change – barroom‑blues energy on songs like Cumberland Blues, Big Railroad Blues and Truckin', plus a jaw‑dropping pedal‑steel‑enhanced Looks Like Rain.  The soundboard source has a few early‑mix quirks (hum, buzz and low‑level Jerry vocals) but overall the mix is clear enough to enjoy the band’s tight playing.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments about performance and song quality, with only minor technical complaints that do not outweigh the musical strengths.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Initial mix has hum and buzz, especially in the first few minutes, and Jerry’s guitar/voice sit low at the start.  Later sections are much clearer; recent cleaning‑up of the mix has improved balance between Bobby and Jerry.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note a strong, bar‑room blues feel that propels the first set, with an energetic second half featuring extended jams (PITB) and tight transitions.  A few comments mention a set‑list ordering glitch, but overall pacing is praised.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone is praised (especially on Looks Like Rain), wah‑wah on Greatest Story gets special love; early mix puts his vocals/guitar low but improves later.
band_member_comments.Phil: Sung lead on Looks Like Rain, described as “Phil rocks” and a highlight of the performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Often front in the early mix (some reviewers call it ‘cheesy’), but vocal parts are still enjoyed.
